binaries.title: msrpc.sys (kernel RPC runtime) - endpoint-map lookup tower-count clamp graduated from WIL-feature-staged to unconditional in EP_LOOKUP_DATA::FreeOldLookupData (defense-in-depth)

changed_functions.name: EP_LOOKUP_DATA::FreeOldLookupData (0x1C0033FEC -> 0x1C00331EC)
key_changes: REMOVED: call to the WIL feature-staging gate Feature_666052921__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage() (reads global Feature_666052921__private_featureState, tests bit 0x10, else routes to wil_details_IsEnabledFallback). In the unpatched code this gate conditionalized the tower-array count clamp. This is a WIL feature flag, NOT a WNF query.

CHANGED: the clamp of the count at this+0x2F8 (count of the tower-pointer array at this+0x2D8, four slots) to a maximum of 4 is now UNCONDITIONAL. Unpatched: `if (feature_enabled && count>4) count=4;` Patched: `if (count>4) count=4;`

Consequence: with the feature disabled, the unpatched free loop's clamp was skipped; if count exceeded 4 the ExFreePoolWithTag loop would read past the 4-slot array (index 4 == the count field at +0x2F8; index 5+ == memory beyond the struct) and free the read-back pointers. The count originates from the endpoint-map RPC reply and is bounded by NDR array validation present in both builds, so count>4 is not demonstrably reachable.

The feature 666052921 is retired in the patched build: its gate, its private descriptor, and the feature-specific WIL staging helper functions are removed, and a global feature-descriptor table shrank (shifting shared type-format lookup tables by 0x10, which accounts for relocation-only diffs in ~56 NDR/WIL functions).
changed_functions.security_notes: EP_LOOKUP_DATA::FreeOldLookupData cleans up a client-side endpoint-map lookup. The object holds a fixed 4-slot tower-pointer array at +0x2D8 (offsets +0x2D8,+0x2E0,+0x2E8,+0x2F0) with its count at +0x2F8. The cleanup loop frees each entry via ExFreePoolWithTag(p,'RpcM'/0x4D637052). Pre-patch, the clamp of the count to 4 ran only when the WIL feature-staging gate Feature_666052921__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage() returned nonzero; when it returned 0 the clamp was skipped (jz over it). The array and count are [out] parameters of the endpoint-map RPC issued in EP_LOOKUP_DATA::LookupNextChunk (this+0x2D8 as the array base, this+0x2F8 as the count pointer, with a hard-coded maximum of 4 passed to NdrClientCall3 / Ndr64AsyncClientCall). The returned tower count is thus supplied by the endpoint-mapper reply but is unmarshalled through the NDR conformant/varying-array path, which validates the returned length against the fixed capacity of 4; that NDR validation is byte-identical in both builds. EP_LOOKUP_DATA::ProcessNextChunk iterates the same array using the same unclamped count in both builds, so the effective bound on the loop is the NDR validation, not this clamp. The patch makes the clamp independent of feature state (defense-in-depth). CWE-125 (out-of-bounds read guarded by the clamp); the free of a read-back pointer is a secondary pool-corruption hazard. No attacker-reachable out-of-bounds or arbitrary-free primitive is demonstrated. Severity: low.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED (EP_LOOKUP_DATA::FreeOldLookupData @ 0x1C0033FEC) - gate + conditional clamp:
  0x1C003406B  call    Feature_666052921__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage  ; WIL feature-staging gate (removed by patch)
  0x1C0034070  test    eax, eax
  0x1C0034072  mov     eax, [rbx+2F8h]        ; tower-array count
  0x1C0034078  jz      0x1C003408B           ; feature disabled -> SKIP clamp
  0x1C003407A  mov     ecx, 4
  0x1C003407F  cmp     eax, ecx
  0x1C0034081  jbe     0x1C003408B           ; count<=4 -> skip
  0x1C0034083  mov     [rbx+2F8h], ecx       ; count = 4 (clamp, gated)
  0x1C0034089  mov     eax, ecx
  ; free loop over the 4-slot array at +0x2D8 (bound = count):
  0x1C0034091  mov     rcx, [rbx+rdi*8+2D8h] ; array slot (OOB if edi>=4)
  0x1C0034099  test    rcx, rcx
  0x1C003409C  jz      0x1C00340AF
  0x1C003409E  mov     edx, 4D637052h        ; 'RpcM'
  0x1C00340A3  call    cs:__imp_ExFreePoolWithTag
  0x1C00340BA  cmp     edi, [rbx+2F8h]       ; loop bound = count
  0x1C00340C0  jb      0x1C0034091

PATCHED (EP_LOOKUP_DATA::FreeOldLookupData @ 0x1C00331EC) - gate removed, clamp unconditional:
  0x1C003326B  mov     eax, [rbx+2F8h]
  0x1C0033271  mov     ecx, 4
  0x1C0033276  cmp     eax, ecx
  0x1C0033278  jbe     0x1C0033282
  0x1C003327A  mov     [rbx+2F8h], ecx       ; ALWAYS clamp to 4
  0x1C0033280  mov     eax, ecx

removed: Feature_666052921__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage and its WIL feature-staging helper functions (feature 666052921 retirement): Feature_666052921__private_IsEnabledFallback, wil_details_IsEnabledFallback, wil_details_StagingConfig_Load, wil_details_StagingConfig_QueryFeatureState, wil_StagingConfig_QueryFeatureState, wil_RtlStagingConfig_QueryFeatureState, wil_details_FeatureStateCache_ReevaluateCachedFeatureEnabledState, wil_details_FeatureStateCache_TryEnableDeviceUsageFastPath, wil_details_GetCurrentFeatureEnabledState, wil_details_FeatureReporting_IncrementOpportunityInCache, wil_details_FeatureReporting_IncrementUsageInCache, wil_details_FeatureReporting_RecordUsageInCache, wil_details_FeatureReporting_ReportUsageToService, wil_details_FeatureReporting_ReportUsageToServiceDirect, wil_details_MapReportingKind, wil_details_StagingConfigFeature_HasUniqueState

security_findings.entry_point: Kernel-mode RPC client endpoint resolution. A client binding that needs a dynamic endpoint drives ResolveEndpointWithEpMapper (0x1C0031B04) -> EP_LOOKUP_DATA::ResolveEndpoint (0x1C0030CE8) -> EP_LOOKUP_DATA::LookupNextChunk (0x1C0031288), which issues the endpoint-map RPC and fills the tower array/count; cleanup runs EP_LOOKUP_DATA::FreeOldLookupData. Not reachable as a direct user-mode-controlled OOB: the count comes from the endpoint-mapper reply and is bounded by NDR array validation.
call_chain: EP_LOOKUP_DATA::LookupNextChunk (0x1C0031288) - issues endpoint-map RPC (NdrClientCall3 / Ndr64AsyncClientCall) with array at this+0x2D8, count at this+0x2F8, max = 4
EP_LOOKUP_DATA::ProcessNextChunk (0x1C0030F18) - consumes towers (iterates count, unclamped in both builds)
EP_LOOKUP_DATA::FreeOldLookupData (0x1C0033FEC) - frees the array; clamp on count was WIL-feature-gated pre-patch
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Conditional bounds clamp made unconditional; guards an out-of-bounds read (CWE-125) whose read-back pointers feed ExFreePoolWithTag. Defense-in-depth hardening, not a demonstrated reachable primitive.
security_findings.description: EP_LOOKUP_DATA::FreeOldLookupData frees the entries of a fixed 4-slot tower-pointer array at this+0x2D8, iterating i=0..count where count = *(uint32)(this+0x2F8). Pre-patch, the defensive clamp `if (count>4) count=4` ran only when the WIL feature-staging gate Feature_666052921__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage() returned nonzero; when it returned 0 the clamp was skipped. If count exceeded 4 in that state, the loop would read array slots past index 3 (index 4 re-reads the count field at +0x2F8, indices 5+ read bytes beyond the object) and pass any non-zero value to ExFreePoolWithTag(p,'RpcM'). The count is written only by NDR unmarshalling of the endpoint-map reply into the fixed 4-entry [out] array, whose returned length is validated by the NDR conformant/varying-array path (identical in both builds), so count>4 is not demonstrably reachable. The patch removes the gate and makes the clamp unconditional. The gate is Windows WIL feature-staging machinery, not a WNF query; feature 666052921 is retired in the patched build.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: 1) Endpoint resolution reached (a client binding resolving a dynamic endpoint via the endpoint mapper). 2) Feature_666052921__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage() returns 0 so the FreeOldLookupData clamp is skipped. 3) this+0x2F8 > 4 at cleanup time. Condition 3 is the limiting factor: the count comes from the endpoint-map reply and is bounded to the fixed capacity of 4 by NDR array validation present in both builds. No path was found that stores this+0x2F8 > 4 past that validation, and EP_LOOKUP_DATA::ProcessNextChunk iterates the same array with the unclamped count in both builds, so the delivered clamp is not the load-bearing bound.
security_findings.primitive: None demonstrated. If the count could be driven above 4 (requires bypassing NDR array validation that exists in both builds), the loop would perform a kernel out-of-bounds read of adjacent pool memory and free the read-back pointers via ExFreePoolWithTag, a pool-corruption hazard. This is guarded defense-in-depth; no attacker-reachable out-of-bounds or arbitrary-free primitive is shown.
security_findings.assembly:
EP_LOOKUP_DATA::FreeOldLookupData @ 0x1C0033FEC (UNPATCHED, verbatim):
  0x1C0033FEC  mov     [rsp+arg_0], rbx
  0x1C0033FF1  mov     [rsp+arg_8], rsi
  0x1C0033FF6  push    rdi
  0x1C0033FF7  sub     rsp, 20h
  0x1C0033FFB  xor     edi, edi
  0x1C0033FFD  mov     rbx, rcx
  ; first array cleanup (+0xB4 count, +0xC0 array), identical in both builds:
  0x1C0034000  cmp     [rcx+0B4h], edi
  0x1C0034006  jbe     0x1C0034039
  0x1C0034008  mov     rcx, [rbx+rdi*8+0C0h]
  0x1C0034010  test    rcx, rcx
  0x1C0034013  jz      0x1C003402F
  0x1C0034015  mov     edx, 4D637052h
  0x1C003401A  call    cs:__imp_ExFreePoolWithTag
  0x1C0034026  and     qword ptr [rbx+rdi*8+0C0h], 0
  0x1C003402F  inc     edi
  0x1C0034031  cmp     edi, [rbx+0B4h]
  0x1C0034037  jb      0x1C0034008
  0x1C0034039  and     dword ptr [rbx+0B4h], 0
  0x1C0034040  lea     rcx, [rbx+2C8h]
  0x1C0034047  cmp     qword ptr [rcx], 0
  0x1C003404B  jz      0x1C0034052
  0x1C003404D  call    RpcSsDestroyClientContext
  0x1C0034052  lea     rdi, [rbx+2C0h]
  0x1C0034059  cmp     qword ptr [rdi], 0
  0x1C003405D  jz      0x1C003406B
  0x1C003405F  mov     rcx, rdi
  0x1C0034062  call    RpcBindingFree
  0x1C0034067  and     qword ptr [rdi], 0
  ; WIL feature gate + conditional clamp:
  0x1C003406B  call    Feature_666052921__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage  ; *** removed by patch ***
  0x1C0034070  test    eax, eax
  0x1C0034072  mov     eax, [rbx+2F8h]        ; tower-array count
  0x1C0034078  jz      0x1C003408B           ; *** feature disabled -> SKIP clamp ***
  0x1C003407A  mov     ecx, 4
  0x1C003407F  cmp     eax, ecx
  0x1C0034081  jbe     0x1C003408B           ; count<=4 -> skip
  0x1C0034083  mov     [rbx+2F8h], ecx       ; count = 4 (clamp, gated)
  0x1C0034089  mov     eax, ecx
  ; free loop over the 4-slot array at +0x2D8:
  0x1C003408B  xor     edi, edi
  0x1C003408D  test    eax, eax
  0x1C003408F  jz      0x1C00340C2
  0x1C0034091  mov     rcx, [rbx+rdi*8+2D8h] ; array slot (OOB if edi>=4)
  0x1C0034099  test    rcx, rcx
  0x1C003409C  jz      0x1C00340AF
  0x1C003409E  mov     edx, 4D637052h        ; 'RpcM'
  0x1C00340A3  call    cs:__imp_ExFreePoolWithTag
  0x1C00340AF  and     qword ptr [rbx+rdi*8+2D8h], 0
  0x1C00340B8  inc     edi
  0x1C00340BA  cmp     edi, [rbx+2F8h]       ; loop bound = count
  0x1C00340C0  jb      0x1C0034091
  ; tail: single aux ptr at +0x2D0 (identical in both builds):
  0x1C00340C2  mov     rcx, [rbx+2D0h]
  0x1C00340C9  test    rcx, rcx
  0x1C00340CC  jz      0x1C00340E7
  0x1C00340CE  mov     edx, 4D637052h
  0x1C00340D3  call    cs:__imp_ExFreePoolWithTag
  0x1C00340DF  and     qword ptr [rbx+2D0h], 0
  0x1C00340E7  mov     rbx, [rsp+28h+arg_0]
  0x1C00340EC  mov     rsi, [rsp+28h+arg_8]
  0x1C00340F1  add     rsp, 20h
  0x1C00340F5  pop     rdi
  0x1C00340F6  retn

breakpoints.why: Cleanup of the endpoint-map lookup. rcx (arg1) = the EP_LOOKUP_DATA object. Inspect [rcx+0x2F8] (tower count) and the four slots [rcx+0x2D8..0x2F0]. On the normal path [rcx+0x2F8] is <= 4.

The WIL feature-staging gate. eax on return determines whether the clamp runs: eax==0 skips the clamp (jz 0x1C003408B taken). Reads global Feature_666052921__private_featureState bit 0x10, else routes to wil_details_IsEnabledFallback. This is a WIL feature flag, not a WNF query.
Issues the endpoint-map RPC that fills the tower array at this+0x2D8 and the count at this+0x2F8 (max 4). Confirm the returned count is bounded to <= 4 by NDR array validation.

watch: [rbx+0x2F8] dword = tower-array count; on the normal path bounded to <= 4 by NDR
rdi / edi = free-loop index; values >= 4 would be out of bounds (array has slots 0..3)
rcx at 0x1C0034091 = pointer read from the array slot, passed to ExFreePoolWithTag at 0x1C00340A3
eax at 0x1C0034070 (return of the WIL gate) = 0 means the clamp is skipped
key_offsets: 0x1C003406B  call Feature_666052921__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage  (WIL gate REMOVED by the patch)
0x1C0034078  jz 0x1C003408B       (skips the clamp when the feature evaluates disabled)
0x1C003407A..0x1C0034081  the count=min(count,4) block, only reached when the feature is enabled
0x1C0034091  mov rcx,[rbx+rdi*8+0x2D8]  (free-loop array read; OOB only if count>4)
0x1C00340A3  call ExFreePoolWithTag  (frees the array entry with tag 'RpcM')
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Drive kernel-mode RPC client endpoint resolution (a binding resolving a dynamic endpoint via the endpoint mapper) so EP_LOOKUP_DATA::LookupNextChunk issues the endpoint-map RPC and cleanup calls FreeOldLookupData. To exercise the skipped-clamp branch, Feature_666052921__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage must evaluate disabled. Reaching count>4 additionally requires bypassing the NDR conformant/varying-array validation that bounds the returned tower count to the fixed capacity of 4 (present in both builds); no such path was found.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: On the normal path [rcx+0x2F8] is <= 4 and the loop stays in bounds. The clamp difference between builds is observable only if the count field is forced above 4, which the NDR path does not allow. If it were, edi would increment to 4 and beyond and ExFreePoolWithTag (0x1C00340A3) would be called on read-back pool bytes, risking BAD_POOL_CALLER (0xC2) / BAD_POOL_HEADER (0x19) or silent pool corruption with the 'RpcM' (0x4D637052) tag.
debugging_aids.notes: EP_LOOKUP_DATA offsets of interest: +0xB4 first array count (separate, identically-bounded loop); +0xC0 first pointer array; +0x2C0 RpcBindingFree handle; +0x2C8 RpcSsDestroyClientContext handle; +0x2D0 single aux ptr (freed separately); +0x2D8..+0x2F0 the 4-slot tower-pointer array; +0x2F8 count for that array (the clamped field). Freed entries use tag 'RpcM' (0x4D637052). The gate is WIL feature-staging (feature 666052921), retired in the patched build. Addresses are file-relative to image base 0x1C000000; add the module base at runtime.

summary: The patch makes a single logic change: in EP_LOOKUP_DATA::FreeOldLookupData (0x1C0033FEC -> 0x1C00331EC), the clamp that caps the tower-pointer array count at this+0x2F8 to 4 was moved from behind a WIL feature-staging gate (Feature_666052921__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage) to unconditional. The array at this+0x2D8 is a fixed 4 slots freed via ExFreePoolWithTag('RpcM'); its count and the array are [out] parameters of the endpoint-map RPC issued in EP_LOOKUP_DATA::LookupNextChunk (max 4). Because that count comes from the endpoint-mapper reply and is bounded by NDR conformant/varying-array validation present in both builds - and because ProcessNextChunk consumes the same array with the unclamped count in both builds - no attacker-reachable out-of-bounds or arbitrary-free primitive is demonstrated. The gate is Windows WIL feature-staging machinery (not a WNF query); feature 666052921 is retired in the patched build, which also causes relocation-only differences in ~56 NDR/WIL functions (a shared type-format table shifted by 0x10). Assessment: a correct-direction defense-in-depth hardening (CWE-125 out-of-bounds read guarded by an unconditional clamp), low severity. The earlier characterization as a WNF-gated OOB read plus arbitrary ExFreePoolWithTag LPE in an NdrAsyncClientCall client-call context was incorrect: wrong function/object, wrong gate mechanism, wrong CWE direction (read, not write), and an unsupported exploit chain.
